understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to navigate through each level by reaching the goal gem tile marked '+' while avoiding spikes and utilizing clicks to remove breakable blocks. I needed to manage my limited turns (50 per attempt, 2 attempts per level) efficiently to complete levels.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was primarily horizontal (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT), after which gravity would pull the character in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid tile, hitting a hazard, or reaching the goal. Gravity could be DOWN (falling toward lower y values/top of screen) or UP (falling toward higher y values/bottom). CLICK actions removed breakable tiles ('x') at specified positions and did not consume a turn from the moving platform timer. Spikes ('u'/'v') caused instant failure upon landing. UNDO allowed reversing the last action. The 'corridor_scan' and landing previews showed where the player would end up after moving left or right, helping identify passages to different y-levels. The moving platform timer (when present) counted down only with horizontal moves, not clicks.
